Clarity.justification: The generated diagram is significantly more complex and includes many additional components and connections not present in the ground truth. This complexity can make it difficult for non-technical stakeholders to understand. The layout is also more cluttered compared to the simple and clear structure of the ground truth diagram.

Completeness.justification: The generated diagram includes many additional elements and connections that are not present in the ground truth. It also lacks the clear and simple structure of the ground truth, which focuses on a single node with four components.

Consistency.justification: The generated diagram uses different styles, symbols, and notations compared to the ground truth. The ground truth diagram is simple and uses a consistent style, whereas the generated diagram is more complex and lacks uniformity in its representation.
